title: eugenia cordata var. cordata
intro: Eugenia cordata var. cordata, also known as the Panama rose, is a variety of flowering plant in the myrtle family that is native to Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Eugenia cordata var. cordata include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems, thanks to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Eugenia cordata var. cordata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies to make infusions and salves to aid in wound healing and skin care.
